buildlog: Batch (#447/#448/#449) — cutting fixed governance ceremony on small changes. #447: per-merge BUILDLOG entries are harvested from a pre-merge `<!-- buildlog-entry -->` PR comment into the ledger-branch rendered log; the separate bookkeeping PR is retired and `BUILDLOG.md` on main keeps only [HALT]/[AUDIT]/wave notes (frozen history in governance/buildlog-history.md). #448: a trivial commit-gate path lets a hand-built dep bump the classifier already rates `auto` ship on green CI, recomputed from the staged tree (dep-version-only diff, fail-closed, tier rules single-sourced in classify-dep-pr-core.ps1). #449: the governance-ledger comment is generated from persisted review evidence (emit-ledger-comment.ps1). System-level review PASS on tree 6e5f523a (reviewer-pr x2 + design-philosophy) after fixing a duplicated marker, a package.json-scope hole in the trivial gate, and a CI cross-platform hook-launcher gap.

Triaged and dismissed CodeQL alert #71 (`js/missing-rate-limiting`, high) — a fresh instance of the accepted won't-fix disposition on the #338 guest comment routes (`POST /p/:submissionId/comments` and `.../comments/:commentId/delete`, both `requireGuest`-gated), superseded by #283. Added a dated entry to `docs/security/codeql-triage-2026-06.md`; alert dismissed via `gh api`. No source change. Graduated from backlog #422.

#399 — Retired four stale "private link needed" comments left by #241's `requireGuest` redirect to `/join`. Comment-only across `src/app.js`, `src/views/partials/message-card.ejs`, and the AC blocks of `tests/bug-reports.test.js` and `tests/how-to-play.test.js`; the `message-card.ejs` 403 case was re-labeled to the surviving comment-ownership refusal (community.js, from #338) rather than dropped. No behavior change.

Fixed the guest masthead nav overflowing horizontally at iPhone-SE-class widths (#388) — `.site-nav-row` now wraps instead of forcing the whole page to scroll sideways below 430px. Bundled with a spacing fix on `/tasks` (#471): the "Share a memory" button had no top margin and sat flush against the task list. Both owner-approved via the visual-approval loop at 320/375/412/430px.

#242 — Guest sessions now last 400 days with a rolling refresh on every authenticated request, so a guest who signs up weeks before the wedding is still signed in on the day without doing anything; the admin cookie stays at its unchanged 14 days. `config.js` now hard-fails at boot in production when `COOKIE_SECRET` is unset, closing the footgun where a regenerated secret silently signs out every guest and admin at once mid-event (dev/test keep the warn-and-generate fallback). Both cookie lifetimes are centralized in `config.js` and `cookieOpts()` is single-owned in `src/middleware/session.js`.

**#467 — trivial dep-bump classifier now inspects package-lock.json content.** The `chore(deps):` trivial-commit waiver only checked the lockfile's staged path, never its content, so a trivial commit could smuggle an unreviewed lockfile repin of a wedding-critical package past both kernel gates. Added condition 4 to `classify-trivial-commit.ps1`: every added/changed/removed key in the lockfile `packages` map must belong to the manifest-changed dep set (or the confined root `""` entry); anything else — including an unparsable or `packages`-less lockfile — fails closed to `standard`. Transitive-dependency drift is deliberately rejected. The lockfile parser is edition-aware: `JavaScriptSerializer` on Windows PowerShell 5.1 (dodges its empty-key `ConvertFrom-Json` throw) and `ConvertFrom-Json -AsHashtable` on PowerShell 7 Core (where the .NET Framework serializer does not exist) — a Linux-CI-only failure the first pass missed. Reviewed at the security-flagged + system-level bar (3× reviewer-pr + design-philosophy, all PASS across two rounds).

**#474 — PR-path reviewers now emit a machine-readable JSON verdict block.** `reviewer-pr` and `reviewer-design-philosophy` emit a complete `review-verdict.schema.md` object (`reviewerId` from the spawn prompt, `verdict`, `defects[]`) after their prose review — additive, not a replacement. This is the deferred prose→JSON slice that #455 needs: once wired, `review-runner.ps1` records verdict evidence captured verbatim from the reviewer's own output instead of orchestrator transcription. `tests/review-runner-conformance.test.js` proves the emitted shape is runner-consumable (the #474↔#455 seam). Issue-stage reviewers stay prose. System-level review: 2× reviewer-pr + design-philosophy PASS.

**#455 — review-runner wired into the PR-review recording path.** Adds `tools/capture-reviewer-verdict.ps1`, which extracts each PR-path reviewer's own emitted JSON verdict block (per #474) and writes it verbatim to the runner's RunDir; the orchestrator now records PR-review evidence via capture→`review-runner.ps1` instead of a hand `persist-review.ps1` call, and the hand-write fallback is removed. Step 6 mandates a distinct `reviewerId` per reviewer so captured blocks don't collide (a seam gap dogfooding caught after round 1 passed). This closes the self-attestation residual for the PR-review path — a recorded PASS is now fed from the reviewer agent's own returned bytes, not orchestrator transcription. The PR recorded its own verdict through the new path. Issue-review path and operator-machine-control caveat remain open, documented honestly.

**#244 — Retire per-guest QR/token entry for one shared poster link.** `GET /j/:token` and `/onboard` now 302 to `/join` (no cookie); admin guest-creation (`POST /admin/guests`, `/guests/bulk`) and the per-guest QR place-card sheet (`/admin/qrsheet`) are removed and 404; new `GET /admin/poster` prints one shared QR pointing at `/join`. `guests.token` kept as the internal-only session credential inside the `gsid` cookie — never distributed. ~40 tests plus the CI smoke gate and load-test tool migrated off `/j/` sign-in to a signed-`gsid`-cookie helper; the smoke #187 hostile-avatar probe moved to `POST /me/edit`. Docs + DESIGN.md updated. System-level review: 2× reviewer-pr PASS + reviewer-design-philosophy PASS, bias-gate PASS, issue-review PASS.

**#255 — task-complete success card + badge-earned modal.** A task completion now lands as a moment. Every `created` submission shows an inline success card (check, `Task complete!`, the fresh points total, share-a-memory). When it earns a new badge, a native `<dialog>` modal auto-opens: an eyebrow, the badge's own name as the title (`First Bloom!`), the large badge art blooming in — a springy pop with a staggered petal burst, glow ring, and breeze sway, all gated under `prefers-reduced-motion` — the badge's catalog description as subtitle, and a `Continue` button. Copy and styling come entirely from the design system and the badge catalog (no invented text); modal CSS is scoped under `.badge-dialog` so it can't collide with the profile badge grid. Reward delivery required fixing a real user-facing bug: the AJAX photo upload (`upload.js`) followed the server redirect with `fetch`, silently consuming the one-shot reward/flash cookie so the card, modal, and even upload-error flashes never appeared in a real browser — now fixed with `redirect: 'manual'` + a full navigation. Owner-approved live; Goal B — instant rewards.

fix(export) #462 — Keepsake export no longer silently loses memory photos. Memory submissions (task_id NULL) all resolved to the same ZIP entry name `task-0-task-null.<ext>`, so a guest's second-and-later memories were dropped on extraction — a Goal D ("one shared record, kept") failure. Memory entries are now named `memory-<submissionId>` (unique per row) and the summary sheet labels them `Memory` instead of `Task #null`. Adds export-zip tests that parse the real ZIP central directory to prove distinct, non-colliding names archive-wide.

#339 — drop trailing Done section from Tasks to-do view: the Tasks page's default (to-do) view rendered a trailing "Done" section — three most recent completions plus a "see all" link — below the to-do list, even though the `Done · N` filter chip already existed as the dedicated way to see finished tasks; a guest tapping "To do" still scrolled into done tasks, making the filter look ignored. Removed the trailing section; the Done chip (`?view=done`) is unchanged. Sonnet-only tier (routine paths, no wedding-critical guest surface, no schema change) — issue review, PR review, and design-philosophy review all PASSed on Sonnet.
<!-- /buildlog-entry -->

**#463 — Bounded HEIC sniff on the upload path (Goal A).** `resolveUploadedFile` no longer reads the whole uploaded file (up to 15 MB) into memory on every submission just to check a 12-byte type marker. It now sniffs only the leading 12 bytes via a bounded `openSync`/`readSync`/`closeSync`, and reads the full file only inside the HEIC-confirmed branch, where the decoder actually needs the bytes. This removes the per-upload main-thread read that serialized the event loop under a reception-night upload burst — one of the concrete peak mitigations #311 called for. Behavior is unchanged for non-HEIC, HEIC, and short/empty files; covered by a byte-for-byte identity test (AC1) and a structural guard on the read placement (AC3).

**Most Liked badge (#484).** Added `MOSTLIKED`, a steal-able system badge held by the guest(s) whose visible photos have collected the most total likes (summed per guest, a sibling of Most Photos). Ties are held together, a guest with zero likes never holds it, and a taken-down photo's likes drop out of the owner's total. The like/unlike route now recomputes transferable badges so the holder set moves as likes come in. Ships with owner-approved badge art in the house palette. Scoped as a per-guest total, not the single most-liked photo — the most-liked-*photo* medal is tracked separately for after the photo-badge substrate (#483) lands.

**#483 — task badges: model, upload slot, award points/note/photo.** Foundation of the badge system. Every task now owns its own `badges` row (`type='custom'`, `code='TASK-<id>'`), so awarding a task's badge to a photo never collides on `guest_badges UNIQUE(guest_id, badge_id)`; un-customized tasks share the default-ribbon SVG art, hosts upload art+name from the task board. Awards carry `points`/`note`/`submission_id` on `guest_badges`, and award points are counted only while the earning photo is visible (takedown-guarded, mirroring `photo_bonus`) via a correlated subquery — no leaderboard fan-out. `createCustomBadge` refuses the reserved `TASK-` prefix. New `src/services/task-badges.js` is the sole task-badge write path; `saveBadgeArt` mirrors the avatar sharp pipeline. Owner-approved visuals at three phone form factors; system-level two-reviewer + design-philosophy PASS; security lens advisory PASS.

#498 — fix(badges): swap in redesigned Completionist artwork. Replaced the double-fern placeholder in `src/public/badges/completionist.svg` with the owner's checkmark-in-circle redesign (confetti/vine accents). The owner's source export used a `<defs><style>` block with CSS classes (`.st0`–`.st3`) — banned by `tests/badge-catalog.test.js`'s `#193 AC2` — and dropped the `role="img" aria-label="Completionist badge"` convention every other badge SVG carries; both fixed by re-expressing the same colors and geometry as inline presentation attributes, no visual or geometric change. Issue review round 1 FAILed on a real defect: the draft described the messy markup as already committed, when it was actually only an uncommitted file in the owner's primary checkout never copied into the isolated worktree — round 2 corrected the before/after and embedded the exact literal target content for the implementer. `reviewer-pr` PASS (0 defects, all 4 ACs traced input→output) + `reviewer-design-philosophy` PASS (0 blocker/major — two low-confidence minors on color-grouping and missing explicit width/height, neither blocking) on tree `8df3be965d7`. `npm test -- tests/badge-catalog.test.js` 15/15 green. **Visual change — owner approved at three phone form factors** (iPhone SE / iPhone 14 Pro Max / Galaxy S20 Ultra), rendered via a scratch-seeded guest holding the badge. Sonnet-only run tier (static asset, no wedding-critical path, no schema change). Merge-on-green, single review round.
